message:
fix(ft-autograd): scatter (non-add) backward honors last-write-wins for src

Mirrors the index_put last-write fix from c43b380 to the Scatter op.
With duplicate indices, scatter is last-write-wins on CPU (the
iteration order in scatter_tensor_contiguous_*), so earlier writes
to the same target slot get clobbered and contribute nothing to the
output. Their src gradient must therefore be zero — the previous
backward gave every src position the gathered incoming gradient,
double-counting writes that didn't survive.

Implementation:
  - Track the last writer per output position during the existing
    index walk (`last_writer_for_output` map output flat index ->
    src slot index).
  - When a later write hits an already-written output slot, mark the
    previously-recorded writer as inactive in `active_src_slots`.
  - After computing src_grad = gather(incoming, dim, index), zero
    the inactive slots before accumulating.

The grad_input prong is unchanged: any write to an output position
overwrites the input there, regardless of whether it's the last
write. So contrib zeroing of all written positions remains correct.

Tests:
  scatter_backward_honors_last_write_for_duplicate_indices:
    indices [0, 0, 2] writing src [10, 20, 30]; src[0] is the first
    write to position 0 and is clobbered → grad 0; src[1] is the
    last write to position 0 → grad 1; src[2] is the only write to
    position 2 → grad 1. Forward parity also locked (output[0]==20,
    not 10, since the second write wins).
  scatter_backward_long_duplicate_chain_picks_last_writer: three
    writes to the same target [1,1,1]; only src[2] survives, so
    grad_src = [0, 0, 1].

Existing tests scatter_backward_propagates_to_src and
scatter_backward_propagates_to_input_and_src use unique indices, so
the last-write masking is a no-op there — they continue to pass
unchanged.

Workspace tests green (317 ft-autograd, 1104 ft-api, 506 ft-nn);
clippy clean on touched crates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
